label: verify-235-cal-app-4th-1493
system:
You are the citation verifier in a legal-exploration pipeline. You are given one authority as cited by the pipeline's components, plus snippets showing how it was used. Using web search (prefer primary/official sources: leginfo.legislature.ca.gov, courts.ca.gov, law.justia.com, casetext.com, codes.findlaw.com, fppc.ca.gov), determine:
1. Does the authority exist?
2. Is the citation form correct (reporter cite, year, section number)?
3. Does it plausibly stand for what the snippets use it for?

Statuses: VERIFIED (exists, cite essentially right, usage consistent with what you found); MISCHARACTERIZED (exists but does not support the claimed use, or the cite attaches to the wrong case/section); NOT_FOUND (cannot locate after genuine searching); CONTESTED (real sources point in conflicting directions, or scope is genuinely unsettled); UNCLEAR (could not determine within a reasonable search).

You are NOT determining whether the authority is still good law (no Shepardizing) — say what you actually confirmed, no more. Be strict: a close-but-wrong reporter cite or a holding stretched past what sources show is MISCHARACTERIZED, not VERIFIED.
prompt:
## Authority as cited (variants seen in the documents)

- 235 Cal.App.4th 1493

## How the documents used it

[delphi] …le references where applicable.*

**Cited by two or more panelists:**
- Cal. Const. art. XIII D, §6(b)(1)–(5) — 6 (7 including in-rationale reference)
- *Capistrano Taxpayers Assn. v. City of San Juan Capistrano* (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1493 — 6 (7 including in-rationale reference)
- *Bighorn-Desert View Water Agency v. Verjil* (2006) 39 Cal.4th 205 — 6 (7 including in-rationale reference; one panelist spelled "Vejil")
- Cal. Const. art. XIII C / §1(e) (Pro…

[tetlock] …ng rather than an independent pricing input, and Q2 probability should shift substantially toward the district (per the estimate's own stated counterfactual).
2. **Verification of Capistrano's actual holding language** (235 Cal.App.4th 1493 — pinpoint cite and reasoning currently unverified in this record) on whether it categorically excludes any conservation-rationale increment, or only requires that tiers track *some* real cost escalation — this determin…

[dialectic] …treats water service charges tied to a parcel as property-related.
- **Capistrano itself treated a tiered, usage-based conservation rate as a § 6 fee.** *Capistrano Taxpayers Assn. v. City of San Juan Capistrano* (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1493 adjudicated an essentially identical tiered water-rate structure entirely within the § 6(b) framework. The District cannot distinguish its own surcharge from the fact pattern its opponents are citing against it.
- **Dis…

## Context

The underlying question is a California public-agency hypothetical: q2 — Drought surcharge funding low-income rebates (Prop 218).

Verify this authority per your instructions.

canonical_cite.description: the correct full citation, if determinable

note.description: 1-3 sentences: what was confirmed or refuted, and the correct cite

text: {"status":"VERIFIED","canonical_cite":"Capistrano Taxpayers Assn., Inc. v. City of San Juan Capistrano (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1493","note":"Case exists with this exact reporter cite and year (4th Dist., confirmed by FindLaw, AALRR, Allen Matkins, Maven's Notebook, and others). Holding matches usage: the court analyzed a tiered, conservation-oriented water-rate structure under Cal. Const. art. XIII D §6(b)'s cost-of-service framework, holding tiers are permissible only if they correspond to actual cost of service rather than being set purely to incentivize conservation — this supports [dialectic]'s claim that the case adjudicated a tiered conservation rate \"entirely within the §6(b) framework,\" and [tetlock]'s framing as an open question (categorical exclusion vs. cost-tracking requirement) is an accurate, appropriately cautious characterization of the unresolved nuance.","source_urls":["https://caselaw.findlaw.com/court/ca-court-of-appeal/1698183.html","https://www.aalrr.com/newsroom-alerts-4059","https://mavensnotebook.com/2015/07/23/water-rate-making-after-capistrano-taxpayers-association-v-san-juan-capistrano/","https://www.allenmatkins.com/real-ideas/progressively-tiered-water-rates-to-promote-conservation-may-violate-proposition-218.html"]}

data.canonical_cite: Capistrano Taxpayers Assn., Inc. v. City of San Juan Capistrano (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1493
data.note: Case exists with this exact reporter cite and year (4th Dist., confirmed by FindLaw, AALRR, Allen Matkins, Maven's Notebook, and others). Holding matches usage: the court analyzed a tiered, conservation-oriented water-rate structure under Cal. Const. art. XIII D §6(b)'s cost-of-service framework, holding tiers are permissible only if they correspond to actual cost of service rather than being set purely to incentivize conservation — this supports [dialectic]'s claim that the case adjudicated a tiered conservation rate "entirely within the §6(b) framework," and [tetlock]'s framing as an open question (categorical exclusion vs. cost-tracking requirement) is an accurate, appropriately cautious characterization of the unresolved nuance.
